title: When Evaluating Motivation: - for Others: Ask,
subtitle: Understand Motivations
description:
When evaluating motivation:
- For others: Ask, "Could their actions come from genuine interest, not just external rewards?"
- For yourself: Reflect, "Are my actions really driven by passion, or am I influenced by external factors?"
- Compare honestly: Treat others’ motivations as complex as your own.

Example: A teammate working late might care about the project, not just a bonus.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Observe and Identify Actions
howTo.summary: Spend a few minutes observing a colleague's behavior. Note the specific actions they take and consider the context. This sets the stage for understanding their motivations.

howTo.title: Ask Outward Questions
howTo.summary: Engage in a brief conversation by asking if their actions stem from genuine interest or external rewards. This helps clarify their motivations and fosters open communication.

howTo.title: Reflect on Your Own Motives
howTo.summary: Take time to introspect about your actions. Consider whether they are driven by passion or influenced by external factors. This self-reflection enhances personal awareness.

howTo.title: Log Your Insights
howTo.summary: Document your observations and reflections in a journal. This practice helps track patterns in motivations over time and improves your understanding of intrinsic versus extrinsic drivers.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and insights in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your understanding of motivations. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/intrinsic-vs-extrinsic-motivation-check/.
